outline: Measures of dispersion (range, mean deviation, variance, SD)
Cumulative frequency curve (Ogive); median, quartiles, percentiles

modules.title: Module 1 · Central Tendency
modules.html: Mean: $\bar x = \dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$. Median: middle value when data is arranged in order. Mode: most frequent value.

modules.title: Module 2 · Dispersion
modules.html: Range = max − min. Mean deviation $=\dfrac{\sum|x-\bar x|}{n}$. Variance $\sigma^2 = \dfrac{\sum(x-\bar x)^2}{n}$. Standard deviation $\sigma = \sqrt{\sigma^2}$.

modules.title: Module 3 · Ogive & Quartiles
modules.html:
An ogive plots cumulative frequency against upper class boundaries. From it we can read:

  • Median at cumulative frequency $=\tfrac{N}{2}$.
  • Lower quartile $Q_1$ at $\tfrac{N}{4}$; upper quartile $Q_3$ at $\tfrac{3N}{4}$.
  • The $p$-th percentile at $\tfrac{pN}{100}$.

Interquartile range = $Q_3 - Q_1$.

examples.q: If $Q_1=42$ and $Q_3=68$, find the interquartile range.

quiz.solution: Median = value at cumulative frequency N/2.
